mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions force firms to raise wages and expand hiring, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls signal synchronized demand recovery across durable goods and services sectors, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion phases indicates strong corporate capacity utilization and pricing power, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during late-cycle phases, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative decline in retail sales forecasts exceeding 5 percent signals weakening consumer demand, which forces downward earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the predictive correlation with SPX returns falls below 0.10 over a rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the consumer price index and stagnant federal funds rates compress real interest rates, which increases discount rate risk and valuation uncertainty, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite rising CPI and flat FEDFUNDS, or the interaction effect reverses sign during high-inflation periods, indicating the mechanism is regime-dependent rather than structural.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls provides a continuous income expansion baseline that supports corporate revenue trajectories, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon without requiring threshold crossings.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day forward return distribution shows no positive skew when PAYEMS growth exceeds 100,000, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth in post-shock recovery phases drives rapid earnings revisions upward as capacity constraints ease, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5 percent annualized during recovery phases, or the signal loses predictive power after the first 18 months of the recovery cycle.
